preamble: Spirantization of aspirated stops into fricatives is a well-documented historical process. A classic example is found in the early stages of Grimm's Law, where Proto-Indo-European *p evolved into *pʰ and subsequently into the voiceless labiodental fricative [f].

examples.note: PIE *pǝtēr > English father, through an intermediate aspirated stage *pʰ.
Greek loanwords like 'philosophia' often shifted from [pʰ] to [f] in Latin (filosofia).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
